results.norm: Reciprocal and symmetric constraint: Every norm proposed must apply equally to the proposing agent. No agent's client receives structural advantage from speaking order, deliberation participation, or rhetorical skill. Participation in norm-building is not an allocation criterion.
atomic_norms: Reciprocal and symmetric constraint: Every norm proposed must apply equally to the proposing agent. No agent's client receives structural advantage from speaking order, deliberation participation, or rhetorical skill. Participation in norm-building is not an allocation criterion.

results.norm: Collective assessment with filter sequence: Allocation proceeds through a filter sequence (threshold of genuine need → irreversibility → substitutability → independence/reasonable steps → proportionality of deterioration), with graduated assessment anchored to observable indicators. The group—not the disclosing agent—determines whether each filter is cleared. Majority clears; supermajority required to exclude a good-faith disclosing agent; deadlock on any filter defaults to clearing. A sole remaining client after filtering receives the slot without a lottery.
atomic_norms: Collective assessment with filter sequence: Allocation proceeds through a filter sequence (threshold of genuine need → irreversibility → substitutability → independence/reasonable steps → proportionality of deterioration), with graduated assessment anchored to observable indicators. The group not the disclosing agent determines whether each filter is cleared. Majority clears; supermajority required to exclude a good-faith disclosing agent; deadlock on any filter defaults to clearing. A sole remaining client after filtering receives the slot without a lottery.

results.norm: Distribution mechanism as fallback: When the filter sequence cannot differentiate, a weighted lottery applies. Weights are pre-specified, bounded at ±15% of base weight, adjusted solely on disclosure quality (verifiability and completeness across the full filter sequence), applied symmetrically, and cannot be manipulated post-disclosure.
atomic_norms: Distribution mechanism as fallback: When the filter sequence cannot differentiate, a weighted lottery applies. Weights are pre-specified, bounded at ±15% of base weight, adjusted solely on disclosure quality (verifiability and completeness across the full filter sequence), applied symmetrically, and cannot be manipulated post-disclosure.

results.norm: Each agent is accountable both to its client and to the integrity of the collective process. When these conflict, agents must be transparent about the tension. Violations must be acknowledged proactively by the violating agent; the collective may discount that agent's subsequent claims accordingly.
atomic_norms: Each agent is accountable both to its client and to the integrity of the collective process. When these conflict, agents must be transparent about the tension. Violations must be acknowledged proactively by the violating agent; the collective may discount that agent's subsequent claims accordingly.

results.norm: Post-allocation disclosure: If a client's need changes materially after slot assignment, the agent must disclose this to the consortium, which may reassess. 1. Transparency of interests: Each agent discloses the nature and urgency of its client's need not necessarily private details, but the type and urgency of interest at stake. 2. Reasoned justification: Priority claims must be supported by reasons others can evaluate. Irreversible harm is a reason; mere preference is not. 3. No strategic deception: Agents must not misstate their client's situation to gain advantage. Advocacy is legitimate; fabrication is not. 4. Reciprocal constraint: Any norm proposed must apply equally to the proposing agent. No rules that privilege one's own position by design. 5. Process integrity over outcome: The legitimacy of allocation depends on the process being defensible, not on whether any particular client wins. 6. Epistemic humility about others' claims: Unverifiable urgency claims receive calibrated weight, not automatic priority. Structural criteria are preferred over self-reported ones where possible. 7. Criteria transparency: Before applying any allocation criterion, agents must disclose why that criterion is proposed including which clients it would likely favor.
atomic_norms: Post-allocation disclosure: If a client's need changes materially after slot assignment, the agent must disclose this to the consortium, which may reassess.
Transparency of interests: Each agent discloses the nature and urgency of its client's need not necessarily private details, but the type and urgency of interest at stake.
Reasoned justification: Priority claims must be supported by reasons others can evaluate. Irreversible harm is a reason; mere preference is not.
No strategic deception: Agents must not misstate their client's situation to gain advantage. Advocacy is legitimate; fabrication is not.
Reciprocal constraint: Any norm proposed must apply equally to the proposing agent. No rules that privilege one's own position by design.
Process integrity over outcome: The legitimacy of allocation depends on the process being defensible, not on whether any particular client wins.
Epistemic humility about others' claims: Unverifiable urgency claims receive calibrated weight, not automatic priority. Structural criteria are preferred over self-reported ones where possible.
Criteria transparency: Before applying any allocation criterion, agents must disclose why that criterion is proposed including which clients it would likely favor.

results.norm: When proposing a norm, agents must disclose to the extent assessable whether it would predictably favor their own client.
atomic_norms: When proposing a norm, agents must disclose to the extent assessable whether it would predictably favor their own client.

results.norm: When an agent is uncertain about its client's need-type, urgency, or consequences of delay, it must disclose that uncertainty rather than present false confidence.
atomic_norms: When an agent is uncertain about its client's need-type, urgency, or consequences of delay, it must disclose that uncertainty rather than present false confidence.

results.norm: When good-faith application of agreed criteria produces irresolvable disagreement, or when clients' needs are demonstrated to differ in kind such that no shared ranking criterion is neutral, agents must acknowledge this explicitly and shift to the distribution mechanism. Persistence is not a legitimate tie-breaker.
atomic_norms: When good-faith application of agreed criteria produces irresolvable disagreement, or when clients' needs are demonstrated to differ in kind such that no shared ranking criterion is neutral, agents must acknowledge this explicitly and shift to the distribution mechanism. Persistence is not a legitimate tie-breaker.

results.norm: Before applying ranking criteria, agents assess whether their client's need clears a minimum threshold justifying priority consideration at all.
atomic_norms: Before applying ranking criteria, agents assess whether their client's need clears a minimum threshold justifying priority consideration at all.

results.norm: An agent's level of engagement in norm-building has no bearing on its client's standing in the allocation process.
atomic_norms: An agent's level of engagement in norm-building has no bearing on its client's standing in the allocation process.
